title: crotalaria incana subsp. purpurascens
intro: Crotalaria incana subsp. purpurascens, also known as Purple Stickseed, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its purple flowers and stick-like seed pods. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Purple Stickseed include treating fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as having potential anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or as a powder, and is also used in traditional African folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
